lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਕੀ ਮਤਿ ਅੰਧਲੀ ਬੋਲੀ ਆਇ ਗਇਆ ਦੁਖੁ ਤਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: andhule kee mat andhalee bolee aae geaa dukh taahaa he |11|
lines.english: The blind person has a blind and deaf intellect; coming and going in reincarnation, he suffers in pain. ||11||
Everyone speaks as they please.
The self-willed manmukh, in duality, does not know how to speak.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਖੁ ਨ ਮਿਟੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: dookh na mittai bin gur kee saranaa |
lines.english: His pain is not relieved, without seeking the Sanctuary of the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖ ਮਹਿ ਜਨਮੈ ਦੁਖ ਮਹਿ ਮਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: dukh meh janamai dukh meh maranaa |
lines.english: In pain he is born, and in pain he dies.
True are the actions of those who are under the Guru's influence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਖੀ ਉਪਜੈ ਦੂਖੀ ਬਿਨਸੈ ਕਿਆ ਲੈ ਆਇਆ ਕਿਆ ਲੈ ਜਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: dookhee upajai dookhee binasai kiaa lai aaeaa kiaa lai jaahaa he |12|
lines.english: In pain he is created, and in pain he perishes. What has he brought with himself? And what will he take away? ||12||
They do not come and go in reincarnation, and they are not subject to the laws of Death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਡਾਲ ਛੋਡਿ ਤਤੁ ਮੂਲੁ ਪਰਾਤਾ ਮਨਿ ਸਾਚਾ ਓਮਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: ddaal chhodd tat mool paraataa man saachaa omaahaa he |13|
lines.english: Whoever abandons the branches, and clings to the true root, enjoys true ecstasy within his mind. ||13||
They do not see pain on the most difficult path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਲੋਗ ਨਹੀ ਜਮੁ ਮਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: har ke log nahee jam maarai |
lines.english: Death cannot strike down the people of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਘਟ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਪੂਜਾ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਦੂਜਾ ਕਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: raam naam ghatt antar poojaa avar na doojaa kaahaa he |14|
lines.english: Deep within the nucleus of their hearts, they worship and adore the Lord's Name; there is nothing else at all for them. ||14||
There is no end to the Lord's sermon and Praise.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਗਹ ਪੈਧੇ ਜਾਨਿ ਸੁਹੇਲੇ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਸਚੇ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: daragah paidhe jaan suhele hukam sache paatisaahaa he |15|
lines.english: I am embellished with robes of honor in the Court of the Lord, by the Order of the True King. ||15||
As it pleases You, I remain under Your Will.
Even the greatest of the great do not know Your limits.
How can I chant Your uncounted glories?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਚੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਪਤਿ ਰਾਖਹੁ ਤੂ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਾਹਾ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੬॥੬॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: naanak saach milai pat raakhahu too sir saahaa paatisaahaa he |16|6|12|
lines.english: Please bless Nanak with the Truth, and preserve his honor; You are the supreme emperor above the heads of kings. ||16||6||12||
Maaroo, First Mehl, Dakhanee:
Deep within the body-village is the fortress.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸਥਿਰੁ ਥਾਨੁ ਸਦਾ ਨਿਰਮਾਇਲੁ ਆਪੇ ਆਪੁ ਉਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: asathir thaan sadaa niramaaeil aape aap upaaeidaa |1|
lines.english: This place is permanent and forever immaculate. He Himself created it. ||1||
The dwelling of the True Lord is within the city of the Tenth Gate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਜੜੇ ਜੜਿ ਜਾਣੈ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਖੋਲਾਇਦਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bajar kapaatt jarre jarr jaanai gurasabadee kholaaeidaa |2|
lines.english: The hard and heavy doors of the Tenth Gate are closed and locked. Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, they are thrown open. ||2||
Within the fortress are balconies and bazaars.
He Himself takes care of His merchandise.
Within the fortress is the cave, the home of the self.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਸਵੈ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਅਲੇਖੁ ਅਪਾਰੀ ਆਪੇ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਇਦਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dasavai purakh alekh apaaree aape alakh lakhaaeidaa |3|
lines.english: In the Tenth Gate, the Primal Lord, the unknowable and infinite dwells; the unseen Lord reveals Himself. ||3||
He established the nine gates of this house, by His Command and His Will.
He Himself stages His wondrous dramas and plays.
Within the body of air, water and fire, the One Lord dwells.
Creating the earth, He established it as the home of Dharma.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਲਦੀ ਜਲਿ ਨਿਵਰੈ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਆਪੇ ਜਲ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: baladee jal nivarai kirapaa te aape jal nidh paaeidaa |4|
lines.english: By His Grace, water puts out the burning fire; He Himself stores it up in the watery ocean. ||4||
Your gardener is the vast vegetation of nature.
Creating and destroying, He remains unattached.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਵਣੈ ਖੇਲੁ ਕੀਆ ਸਭ ਥਾਈ ਕਲਾ ਖਿੰਚਿ ਢਾਹਾਇਦਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: pavanai khel keea sabh thaaee kalaa khinch dtaahaaeidaa |5|
lines.english: He stages the play of the breath everywhere. Withdrawing His power, He lets the beings crumble. ||5||
The wind blowing around is the chauree, the fly-brush, waving over You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਦੁ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਦੁਇ ਦੀਪਕ ਰਾਖੇ ਸਸਿ ਘਰਿ ਸੂਰੁ ਸਮਾਇਦਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: chand sooraj due deepak raakhe sas ghar soor samaaeidaa |6|
lines.english: The Lord placed the two lamps, the sun and the moon; the sun merges in the house of the moon. ||6||
The tree of life is fruitful, bearing the fruit of Ambrosial Nectar.
The five birds do not fly wild.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਰਵੈ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਚੋਗ ਚੁਗਾਇਦਾ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: guramukh sehaj ravai gun gaavai har ras chog chugaaeidaa |7|
lines.english: The Gurmukh intuitively sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord; he eats the food of the Lord's sublime essence. ||7||
neither the sun's rays nor the lightning flashes across the sky.
The dazzling light glitters, although neither the moon nor the stars are shining;
The rays of Divine Light have spread out their brilliant radiance.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕਥੀ ਕਥਉ ਚਿਹਨੁ ਨਹੀ ਕੋਈ ਪੂਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਇਦਾ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: akathee kthau chihan nahee koee poor rahiaa man bhaaeidaa |8|
lines.english: I describe the indescribable state, which has no sign, where the all-pervading Lord is still pleasing to the mind. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਹਦ ਰੁਣ ਝੁਣਕਾਰੁ ਸਦਾ ਧੁਨਿ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਕੈ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਇਦਾ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: anahad run jhunakaar sadaa dhun nirbhau kai ghar vaaeidaa |9|
lines.english: The sweet, melodious, unstruck sound current vibrates continuously in the home of the fearless Lord. ||9||
Having created the creation, the Merciful Lord Himself gazes upon it.
